page: Amazon Web Services m6in.32xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 virtual CPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization.

Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and extended instruction tests all ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with read and write speeds placing in the top 25% across both small and large block tests. Single-core performance for tasks like stress-ng and Geekbench falls within the middle 50%, indicating that the instance's strength lies in highly parallelized workloads rather than single-threaded execution speed.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m6in.32xlarge offers high resource density and massive network throughput, featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This makes it highly efficient for network-heavy operations and distributed systems, though the lack of local NVMe storage requires architectural reliance on EBS. The instance is suited for large-scale enterprise applications, multi-threaded database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.32xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 512 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying on its 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity. Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth, though single-core metrics remain average. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized, network-intensive workloads. It is suited for enterprise databases, software compilation, static web serving, and distributed applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-vCPU, network-optimized platform delivering 200 Gbps bandwidth and balanced memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
